vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on Backup gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow-, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow-

triage_decision.reason: The finding involves setting restrictive permissions (0000) on a backup file (/etc/gshadow-). This is a standard hardening practice to prevent unauthorized access to sensitive shadow information and does not affect the operation of the active /etc/gshadow file or system authentication services.
